MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances:
Laurel Halbany of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Defendant has not addressed plaintiff's contention that the flexitallic gaskets were distributed by Lammons. The purpose of the interrogatories is to illicit information possessed by counsel as well as by the client. Any deficiencies in the discovery responses need to be addressed by a motion to compel. The special interrogatory responses were filed in December 2007, long before this motion was filed. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Bretton Morris of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith (262-8573) for defendant and moving party
Jeffrey Wehr of Brayton Purcell (898-1555) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain burden of proof. Defendant has not demonstrated that plaintiff is unable to prove that the bags of insulation described by plaintiff contained asbestos. The Gregory declaration lacks foundation for the statement made. The motion for summary adjudication is denied for defendant's special interrogatories do not require plaintiff to delineate evidence that supports each of plaintiff's causes of action. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
